race_name: RAMROD — Ride Around Mount Rainier in One Day

top_categories.workout_context: 154 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic RAMROD — Ride Around Mount Rainier in One Day's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
At RAMROD — Ride Around Mount Rainier in One Day, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
65ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
RAMROD — Ride Around Mount Rainier in One Day's paved roads circumnavigating Mount Rainier forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 154 miles.
10,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
10,000ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 154 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 10,000ft of climbing on paved roads circumnavigating Mount Rainier does to your power file on race day.
154 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of RAMROD — Ride Around Mount Rainier in One Day. Train it until it feels boring.
Low-cadence force work for 10,000ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 154 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 154 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at RAMROD — Ride Around Mount Rainier in One Day. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Over Under to prepare you for 154 miles of paved roads circumnavigating mount rainier through national park and washington state highways in Enumclaw, Washington, USA.
